Video.summary: Get up close and personal with the creatures that call your backyard home.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.4

Video.summary: When it comes to interacting with our world, nature has nothing to hide.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.9

Video.summary: Get up close and personal with the creatures that call your backyard home.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.4

Video.summary: When it comes to interacting with our world, nature has nothing to hide.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.9

Video.summary: On the last day of their chase the storm hunters are shaken to the core by a giant, unpredictable tornado.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Tornado Terror

Video.summary: On his home turf in Canada, Mark and his team chase one of nature’s deadliest forces, coming face to face with tornadoes’ shear strength.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Shear Strength

Video.summary: From hailstorms to hurricanes and tornadoes, the Storm Hunters revisit their craziest chases and share some of the most amazing moments in extreme weather events.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Best of

Video.summary: Destructive tornadoes and ferocious winds are only two elements Mark and Jaclyn encounter when storm hunting.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Perilous Pursuit

Video.summary: Following an intense hurricane season, Hurricane Michael is poised to hit Florida.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Ferocity in Florida

Video.summary: It’s an ordinary town with a history of heartache. No one knows why Moore, Oklahoma has been the target of 3 mega-tornadoes in just 16 years. On the heels of the May 20, 2013 EF5 tornado, Mark & Jaclyn uncover stories behind the disaster, the recovery and the tenacious spirit of the people of the US Midwest.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Heartache & Hope

Video.summary: On the last day of their chase the storm hunters are shaken to the core by a giant, unpredictable tornado.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Tornado Terror

Video.summary: On his home turf in Canada, Mark and his team chase one of nature’s deadliest forces, coming face to face with tornadoes’ shear strength.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Shear Strength

Video.title: Storm Hunters | Best of

Video.title: Storm Hunters | Perilous Pursuit

Video.title: Storm Hunters | Ferocity in Florida

On the heels of the May 20, 2013 EF5 tornado, Mark & Jaclyn uncover stories behind the disaster, the recovery and the tenacious spirit of the people of the US Midwest.

Video.summary: Josh Morgerman chases devastating Hurricane Maria.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Maria

Video.summary: In 2014, Josh went face to face with one of the most violent hurricanes ever to strike Cabo San Lucas.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Odile

Video.summary: Josh Morgerman chases devastating Hurricane Maria.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Maria

Video.summary: In 2014, Josh went face to face with one of the most violent hurricanes ever to strike Cabo San Lucas.

Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Maria

Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Odile

Video.summary: Here are some encounters that demonstrate that we are at the mercy of nature here on Earth.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Eerie Encounters

Video.summary: Surging waters, brewing storms, icy landscapes, and cosmic phenomena bring the chaos.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Rising Chaos

Video.summary: Here are some encounters that demonstrate that we are at the mercy of nature here on Earth.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Eerie Encounters

Video.summary: Surging waters, brewing storms, icy landscapes, and cosmic phenomena bring the chaos.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Rising Chaos

Video.summary: Here are some encounters that demonstrate that we are at the mercy of nature here on Earth.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Eerie Encounters

Video.summary: Surging waters, brewing storms, icy landscapes, and cosmic phenomena bring the chaos.
Video.title: Sinister Storms | Rising Chaos

Video.summary: For George Kourounis‚ ground zero for stormchasing is Oklahoma City, the capital of´Tornado Alley´.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Tornadoes

Video.summary: The Angry Planet team heads to Africa to climb the steep and very active Nyiragongo Volcano in the Congo that erupted in 2002, sending 125,000 people fleeing for their lives. Host/adventurer George Kourounis explores the bubbling lake of molten lava and investigates the risk the dangerous volcano poses to both the people of city of Goma and the Mountain Gorillas who live on the nearby jungle slopes.
Video.title: Angry Planet | African Hellhole

Video.summary: The autumn of 2006 brings hurricanes to the North Atlantic and hot dry winds fan forest fires across Northern Ontario. Host/adventurer George Kourounis has traveled to some awesome hurricanes, including Rita and Katrina, with his pal Mark Robinson. This year they stormchase in Newfoundland, site of the hurricane that pulled Mark out of a life-threatening depression.
Video.title: Angry Planet | The Winds of Autumn

Image.alt: Angry Planet | The Winds of Autumn
Angry Planet | The Winds of Autumn
Angry Planet | The Winds of Autumn

Video.summary: Host George Kourounis has braved many terrifying phenomena – but none as frightening as the one he takes on in this episode: marriage! To make sure his new bride is on the same adventurous page with him, he proposes to get married on the lip of an exploding volcano, on the South Pacific island of Tanna, in Vanuatu. Before the native preacher, dressed in grass skirt and kustom garb, gets to perform the smoky vows, George descends into the erupting volcano to bring out red hot samples of the lava it is producing.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Volcanic Wedding

Video.summary: Avalanche danger is very high in the Rogers Pass area of the Trans Canada Highway in British Columbia. Host of Angry Planet George Kourounis travels with a variety of experts to bring down huge avalanches in the Rocky Mountains. Ski Patrollers throw hand charges to clear the slopes, the British Columbia Department of Highways uses helicopter bombing and mountaintop mortars to keep the roads safe, and the Canadian Armed Forces fire 105mm howitzers at the mountains to bring down the dangerous snow. Still, there are accidents and fatalities in the mountains. We meet Francois Desroisers, an avalanche burial victim who tells a terrible and tragic tale of a mountain disaster.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Avalanche

Video.summary: Some of the fiercest weather can be experienced at the area the South Americans call the “Fin del Mundo”.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Fin del Mundo

Video.summary: George returns to “Tornado Alley” with 3 fellow stormchasers to see what the 2007 Storm Season will bring.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Midwest Stormfest

Video.summary: One of the most exciting episodes of season one is “Wild Water” – our look at Great White Sharks, water-filled caves and high voltage marine action.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water Two

Video.summary: We hope Season Two will give us the opportunity to make the quintessential Angry Planet show – a full episode exploring a major hurricane that makes landfall in Florida or the Carolinas.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hurricanes '07

Video.summary: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. Eastern flanks of the volcano to slip by up to two metres, and many houses on the flanks of the volcano experienced structural damage. The eruption also completely destroyed the Rifugio Sapienza, on the southern flank of the volcano. The Rifugio was the site of a cable car station which had previously been destroyed in the 1983 eruption; both have now been rebuilt. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Italia Wild

Video.summary: 300 feet from a pocket of molten magma is a chamber filled with giant blue and pink crystals as 60 feet long!
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals

Image.alt: Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals
Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals
Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals

Video.summary: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.
Video.title: Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu

Image.alt: Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu
Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu
Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu

Video.summary: Stormchasing developed in Australia in the same period – the 1990s – as it developed in the American Midwest. In the first week of December – the height of storm season in Australia, we plan to travel with Jimmy Deguara, the most experienced of Australian stormchasers.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Thunder Down Under

Video.summary: In the porous limestone of Canada’s West Coast, great forces are at work under the ground, building and eroding the underground into fantastic shapes. In this episode we’ll witness George’s explorations of these massive underground cave systems.
Video.title: Angry Planet | B.C. Caving

Video.summary: Definitely our wildest episode so far. Here’s the deal: Mt. Elgon is an 14,000 foot volcano in eastern Kenya. We propose a safari/ascent of the dormant volcano. Here’s the first kicker: High on the the flanks of the volcano is Kitum Cave – a cave created by the most unique form of erosion – Elephant Erosion. For millennia elephants have been going into the cave to carve it out with their tusks in order to eat the salty rock. Here’s the second kicker: the cave is thought to be the home of the Ebola Marberg virus – the world’s most deadly virus. So here’s the plan: We are going enter the cave, at night, wearing full Bio-Hazard suits to protect us from the virus.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Kenya

Video.summary: George surfs with the “Hawaian Fire” firefighters.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hawaii
